metadata.title: UAE Contractor Management Services | Jackson & Frank
metadata.description: UAE contractor management and compliant freelancer onboarding. Engage contractors in Dubai and the UAE. Freelance permit, mainland vs free zone rules, invoicing in AED. Fast onboarding support.

faqs.title: UAE Contractor FAQs
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about contractor and freelancer management in the UAE.

items.question: Can foreigners work as contractors in the UAE without an employment visa?
items.answer: Generally, working in the UAE requires a legal basis: employment, a freelance permit in an approved free zone, or a licence that authorises the activity. 'Visitor' status does not replace work authorisation. We help you choose a compliant structure.

items.question: What is the risk of misclassifying employees as contractors?
items.answer: MOHRE and free zone authorities can treat de facto employees as illegally employed or impose fines. Misclassification also creates WPS and visa mismatches. We assess substance and documentation before you engage.

items.question: How fast can I onboard a UAE freelancer?
items.answer: Timelines depend on freelance permit or licence setup and bank KYC. Simple B2B invoicing arrangements where the freelancer already has UAE work rights can be faster; new permits typically take several weeks.

items.question: Can contractors be converted to employees?
items.answer: Yes. When a role should be employment, we can move the person onto UAE EOR with a standard employment contract, visa, insurance, and WPS payroll.

items.question: Do you pay contractors in AED?
items.answer: Yes. AED is standard; other currencies may be agreed where compliant with contract and banking rules.
